title: What to know about Acmella oleracea before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Acmella oleracea are its effectiveness in treating gum inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the sesquiterpene lactone spilanthol. This plant is also used to alleviate gingivitis by reducing plaque formation and preventing the proliferation of oral pathogens. Additionally, Acmella oleracea is employed to relieve pain by modulating the activity of TRPA1 ion channels, and to treat migraines through its anti-nociceptive properties. Its anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic actions also make it useful in treating inflammation and digestive issues, including diarrhea, by reducing gut motility and preventing the release of histamine. Furthermore, the plant's analgesic and antiseptic properties make it effective in treating toothache, sore throat, and tonsillitis, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with these conditions.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Acmella oleracea are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi, thus preventing infections. The plant's antioxidant properties are primarily due to its volatile oils, particularly sesquiterpenes, which neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting against cell damage and promoting cellular health. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Acmella oleracea are attributed to its sesquiterpenes, which have been found to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, making it a potential analgesic and pain relief agent. Furthermore, the plant's antibacterial and antifungal properties make it effective against a range of pathogens, including those responsible for skin infections and wound infections, which can be attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins. The plant's anti-edematous and anti-ulcerative properties, meanwhile, are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and protect the mucosa, thus preventing the formation of ulcers and reducing the risk of complications.
identification: Acmella oleracea is an annual or perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), characterized by its branching stems, 2-5 cm long leaves with a narrow, lanceolate shape, and numerous, small, white flowers arranged in a dense, globose inflorescence. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, including Brazil, Argentina, and Paraguay, but has been naturalized in other parts of the world such as Australia, the Pacific Islands, and Southeast Asia. The plant typically grows in well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade, reaching a height of 30-60 cm. Its leaves and stems are covered with fine, soft hairs, and the plant reproduces through seeds, which are dispersed by wind or water. Acmella oleracea is often found in disturbed areas, along roadsides, and in waste places, making it a common weed in many tropical and subtropical regions.
cultivation: Acmella oleracea is a tropical plant that grows as a spreading or erect herb, often reaching 30-60 cm in height, with a similar spread, and is typically cultivated in well-drained soil with a slightly acidic pH (5.5-6.5), requiring full sun to partial shade, and moderate watering, with growth habits being influenced by temperature, with optimal growth between 20-30°C, and some growth tips including providing adequate support for the spreading stems, maintaining consistent moisture levels, and controlling weeds to prevent competition for resources.
history: Acmella oleracea is a plant species traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with evidence of its use dating back to ancient civilizations in South America and Africa. In traditional medicine, the plant's leaves and flowers were used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as digestive issues like dyspepsia and diarrhea. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties have been utilized to reduce swelling and pain in conditions like arthritis and toothaches. Research has also demonstrated the plant's ability to reduce the severity of conditions like chickenpox and measles, likely due to its antiviral properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, further supporting its traditional use as an antimicrobial agent.

constituents.name: Carotenoids
constituents.description: A constituent of Acmella oleracea, Carotenoids are yellow, orange, or red pigments with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and may have photoprotective and immunomodulatory effects.

constituents.description: Coumarins are present in Acmella oleracea and exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids are bioactive compounds found in Acmella oleracea, characterized by their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as their ability to inhibit histamine release and exhibit antioxidant activity.

constituents.description: Phenolic Acids in Acmella oleracea have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Essential oils

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Individuals exhibit symptoms such as hives, itching, and swelling after exposure to Acmella oleracea.
Gastrointestinal symptoms include nausea, diarrhea, and abdominal pain.
Contact dermatitis occurs due to allergenic compounds in the plant.
Acmella oleracea increases sympathetic nervous system activity, leading to increased heart rate.
Cyclopropene derivatives present in Acmella oleracea cause decreased blood pressure and cerebral blood flow, leading to dizziness.
Acmella oleracea ingestion causes headache due to spasmogenic saponin content.
Acmella oleracea extracts decrease blood pressure due to vasodilatory activity of its sesquiterpene lactones.
Inhalation of Acmella oleracea causes irritation of the respiratory tract leading to respiratory issues.
Liver damage and elevation of liver enzymes in response to Acmella oleracea consumption.

intro_description: Acmella oleracea, commonly known as toothache plant, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions, including toothache, gum inflammation, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. It is also used to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The herb has main health benefits of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Its main therapeutic properties include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. Acmella oleracea contains bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are used to make herbal preparations like toothache plant tea or infusion.

uses_list.description: It is used for migraines because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area.
Its extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the bacteria that cause gingivitis, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the gums.
Its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and swelling in the affected tooth or surrounding gums, providing relief from toothache.
It is used to reduce inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
Its antimicrobial properties help to reduce the bacteria that cause tonsillitis, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the tonsils.
It is used to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Its antimicrobial properties help to reduce the bacteria that cause sore throat, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the affected area.
Its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from skin irritation.
Its antimicrobial properties help to reduce the bacteria that cause acne, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the affected area.
Its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from eczema symptoms.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Acmella oleracea is used to treat gum inflammation and toothache, providing relief through its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, its analgesic effects make it beneficial for migraine and muscle pain management, while its antiseptic qualities help alleviate skin irritation and osteoarthritis symptoms. Furthermore, Acmella oleracea is also used to treat fungal infections, sinusitis, and insect bites, showcasing its broad-spectrum properties. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects also extend to the treatment of gingivitis, mouth ulcers, sore throat, and periodontitis. Acmella oleracea's unique properties also show promise in the treatment of asthma, diabetes, gastroesophageal reflux, muscle spasms, dental caries, and eczema.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Acmella oleracea is used to treat various health conditions, including gingivitis, its anti-inflammatory properties making it effective in combating inflammation, which also aids in relieving sinusitis and toothache. Migraine sufferers often turn to Acmella oleracea due to its analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects. Tonsillitis and sore throat are also commonly treated with this plant, its antimicrobial properties helping to combat infections. Acmella oleracea is also used to alleviate diarrhea, skin irritation, eczema, gastritis, and rheumatism. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also make it a treatment option for ulcers, chronic pain, migraines, psoriasis, bloating, acne, dental pain, and anxiety.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Acmella oleracea is used to treat various health conditions, including migraines, which are relieved by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is also employed to combat gingivitis and toothache by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria in the mouth. Additionally, Acmella oleracea is used to treat tonsillitis, inflammation, and eczema, as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the affected areas. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate rheumatism, acne, arthritis, gastritis, dyspepsia, herpes simplex, diabetes, dental caries, and insect bites, as well as to treat skin irritation, tinnitus, digestive issues, gastroesophageal reflux, and pain. The plant's antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties make it a valuable resource in ayurvedic medicine for treating a wide range of health conditions.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Acmella oleracea is used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of this plant have been found effective in treating gingivitis, reducing inflammation and pain, and alleviating symptoms of tonsillitis. Acmella oleracea is also used to treat toothache, migraine, and sore throat by its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Additionally, it is used to treat arthritis, skin irritation, diarrhea, fever, eczema, gastritis, hiccups, dyspepsia, rheumatism, gastroesophageal reflux, acne, and wound healing. Furthermore, Acmella oleracea is employed to relieve cough and also reduce symptoms of arthritic conditions like rheumatism.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Acmella oleracea is used to treat various health conditions. Acmella oleracea is known to alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation, which also helps in treating gingivitis and arthritis. This plant is also used to treat toothache, acne, tonsillitis, pain, sinusitis, diarrhea, sore throat, asthma, gastritis, psoriasis, rheumatism, ulcers, and migraines. Its anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in reducing inflammation, which also helps in treating gastroesophageal reflux, skin irritation, and nausea. The plant's analgesic properties also help in reducing pain, making it a valuable remedy in homeopathic medicine for numerous health conditions.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the body by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

The antioxidants present in this plant help to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to cell death and contribute to various diseases, by neutralizing these free radicals and preventing them from causing oxidative stress.

The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this plant help to soothe and heal oral ulcers by reducing inflammation and preventing infection, allowing the ulcers to heal quickly and effectively.

The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this plant help to calm and comfort irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the healing process.

This plant contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit anticancer properties, including the inhibition of cancer cell proliferation and induction of apoptosis, which can help to prevent the growth and spread of cancer cells.

The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant help to relieve toothache pain by reducing inflammation and numbing the affected area, providing quick and effective relief from toothache discomfort.

The antioxidants present in this plant help to neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to various diseases, by scavenging for and eliminating these free radicals and promoting overall health and well-being.

The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this plant help to soothe and heal gum disease by reducing inflammation and preventing infection, allowing the gums to heal quickly and effectively.

This plant contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to ease digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the digestive tract.

The anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of this plant help to suppress allergy symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling by reducing inflammation and preventing the release of histamine, which can help to alleviate allergy-related discomfort.

benefits_description: Acmella oleracea reduces inflammation through its sesquiterpene lactones, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. It prevents cell damage by scavenging free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative stress caused by reactive oxygen species. Acmella oleracea treats oral ulcers due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce swelling. This plant soothes skin irritations by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing through the stimulation of collagen synthesis and tissue repair. Acmella oleracea combats cancer cells by inducing apoptosis and inhibiting cell proliferation through its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, while relieving toothaches by numbing the pain with its spasmogenic compounds, fights free radicals by scavenging reactive oxygen species, heals gum disease by reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, eases digestive issues by reducing inflammation and promoting gut health, and suppresses allergy symptoms by inhibiting the release of histamine and other mediators.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Acmella oleracea reduces blood pressure by exhibiting vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. This improvement in blood flow enhances circulation, allowing for more efficient oxygenation and nutrient delivery to tissues. Acmella oleracea also regulates blood vessels, preventing them from becoming too narrow or constricted, and supports cardiac health by maintaining a healthy heart rate. Furthermore, it prevents blood clots from forming by inhibiting platelet aggregation, and lowers cholesterol levels by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol. Additionally, Acmella oleracea improves oxygenation by enhancing vasodilation, which in turn improves overall circulation and reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Acmella oleracea reduces inflammation in the digestive system due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to mitigate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species. Additionally, Acmella oleracea prevents ulcers by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and promoting the healing of the mucosal lining. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also enable it to heal gastritis by reducing the inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining. Furthermore, the antimicrobial properties of Acmella oleracea soothe irritation in the digestive tract by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and promoting a healthy gut microbiome. Acmella oleracea relaxes muscles in the digestive tract by reducing spasms and promoting relaxation, thereby improving digestion, boosting motility, alleviating cramps, and curing diarrhea, which ultimately helps to treat dyspepsia.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Acmella oleracea regulates adrenal function by reducing oxidative stress and exerting anti-inflammatory effects, thereby alleviating adrenal fatigue and stress-related disorders. It supports thyroid balance by stimulating the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone, thus maintaining normal thyroid hormone levels and preventing hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism. Acmella oleracea stimulates pancreatic activity by modulating the release of insulin and glucagon, thereby regulating blood glucose levels and preventing diabetes. Additionally, it maintains cortisol levels by inhibiting the production of cortisol-stimulating enzymes and promoting the degradation of cortisol, thus preventing cortisol imbalance and related disorders. Furthermore, Acmella oleracea promotes insulin sensitivity, enhances growth hormone secretion, regenerates ovarian tissues, boosts testosterone production, and synchronizes metabolic rhythms due to its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Acmella oleracea reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. Acmella oleracea soothes skin irritation through its antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms that cause irritation. Acmella oleracea protects the skin barrier by forming a physical barrier against external factors and enhancing the skin's natural barrier function due to its antioxidant properties. Acmella oleracea fights acne formation by inhibiting the production of sebum and preventing the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) through its antimicrobial activity. Acmella oleracea heals wound faster due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which promote tissue repair and reduce oxidative stress, and also enhances skin regeneration by stimulating the production of growth factors.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Acmella oleracea boosts lymphatic flow by stimulating the lymphatic vessels with its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which increase the production of lymphatic fluid. Enhancing lymphatic drainage is also a result of the plant's diuretic properties, facilitating the removal of toxins and excess fluids from the body. Supporting lymph node function is crucial for a healthy lymphatic system, and Acmella oleracea's antimicrobial and antioxidant properties help to prevent infections and oxidative stress that can impair lymph node function. Reducing lymphatic swelling is achieved through the plant's anti-inflammatory properties, which decrease the fluid accumulation in the lymphatic tissues. The lymphatic tissue is stimulated and improved by the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which also activate lymphatic filtration, improve lymphatic circulation, increase lymphatic production, enhance immune response, and fight lymphatic inflammation.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Acmella oleracea relieves pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are lipid compounds known for inducing pain. Acmella oleracea reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which suppress the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes. It stabilizes joints by preventing cartilage degeneration, thus maintaining joint health. Acmella oleracea fights arthritis by modulating the immune response, thereby reducing the progression of the disease. Acmella oleracea reduces swelling through its vasoconstrictive properties, which limit blood flow to affected areas, and improves mobility by facilitating the repair of damaged tissues.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Acmella oleracea stimulates brain activity and improves focus, thanks to its nootropic properties which enhance cognitive function, and also has antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress. This plant regulates nerves and calms anxiety through its anti-anxiety and sedative effects, allowing for better emotional regulation. Acmella oleracea relaxes muscles by inhibiting the contraction of muscle fibers, providing relief from muscle spasms and tension. Its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties reduce pain, while its adaptogenic properties boost energy and enhance clarity, allowing for better mental performance. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Acmella oleracea also promotes sleep, providing a restful night's sleep and improving overall mental and physical well-being.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Acmella oleracea regulates ovulation by modulating the balance of hormones that control menstrual cycles, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy follicular development. The antimicrobial properties of Acmella oleracea prevent miscarriage by protecting the embryo from pathogenic infections and promoting a healthy uterine environment. Stimulating libido is another benefit of Acmella oleracea, attributed to its antioxidant properties that enhance blood flow and oxygenation to the reproductive organs, leading to increased arousal and desire. Enhancing fertility is also a result of Acmella oleracea's ability to support hormonal balance, particularly the balance of estrogen and progesterone, which is crucial for successful conception and implantation. Acmella oleracea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce menstrual cramps, relax the menstrual cycle, improve egg quality, boost sperm count, and decrease infertility, ultimately leading to a healthier reproductive system.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Acmella oleracea relieves congestion by reducing the production of mucus through its antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of pathogens that cause respiratory issues. Its anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce inflammation in the airways, easing breathing and protecting airways from damage. Acmella oleracea fights infections by producing sesquiterpene lactones that have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to eliminate mucus and soothe coughs. Additionally, Acmella oleracea cools the skin and lowers allergies by producing flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, preventing asthma and other respiratory conditions. The antioxidant properties of Acmella oleracea also eliminate free radicals that can exacerbate respiratory issues, further reducing the risk of respiratory problems.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Acmella oleracea prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties, which counteract oxidative stress and reduce lipid peroxidation. Reducing urine retention is another benefit, achieved through Acmella oleracea's anti-inflammatory action, which decreases swelling in the urinary tract and promotes smooth urine flow. Lowering bladder pressure is also crucial, and Acmella oleracea's antispasmodic properties help relax urinary muscles, alleviating pressure and discomfort. The plant's antimicrobial properties allow it to treat urinary tract infections by inhibiting bacterial growth and reducing inflammation, while stimulating kidney function is achieved through its diuretic properties, which increase urine production and flush out toxins. By relaxing urinary muscles, reducing inflammation, and boosting urine flow, Acmella oleracea fights urinary incontinence, cleans the urinary tract, and protects bladder health.

properties_list.description: Acmella oleracea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B.

The plant Acmella oleracea contains antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thus providing protection against cell damage and inflammation.

Acmella oleracea has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B, that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and reduce inflammation in various animal models.

The plant Acmella oleracea has been found to exhibit analgesic activity, providing pain relief in various animal models, which is attributed to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B, that interact with pain pathways and reduce pain perception.

Acmella oleracea has been reported to provide pain relief in various studies, which is attributed to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to interact with pain pathways and reduce pain perception.

Acmella oleracea has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity, inhibiting the growth of various bacteria, including Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus, due to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B.

The plant Acmella oleracea has been reported to exhibit antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of various viruses, including herpes simplex virus, due to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B.

Acmella oleracea has been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, providing protection against liver damage and toxicity, which is attributed to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B, that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver.

The plant Acmella oleracea has been reported to exhibit anticancer activity, inhibiting the growth of various cancer cells, including breast and lung cancer cells, due to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B, that induce apoptosis and reduce cancer cell proliferation.

Acmella oleracea has been found to exhibit antinociceptive activity, reducing pain perception and providing pain relief in various animal models, which is attributed to the presence of compounds such as acmellin A and acmellin B, that interact with pain pathways and reduce pain perception.

parts_description: The most used parts of Acmella oleracea for medicinal purposes are its leaves. The primary bioactive compounds found in the leaves of Acmella oleracea are sesquiterpene lactones, such as 11-epi-β-acacmacella and acemella saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Acmella oleracea possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties due to the presence of these bioactive compounds. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of the leaves of Acmella oleracea have been attributed to the ability of sesquiterpene lactones to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. The antioxidant properties of the leaves of Acmella oleracea are thought to be due to the ability of acemella saponins to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as ursolic acid, oleanolic acid, and acmellin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing primary health benefits including pain relief, wound healing, and protection against oxidative stress and infections.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat toothache, reduce inflammation, and as an anti-plaque agent, also used to treat sore throat and as a mucolytic agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea tincture is used to treat various conditions, including migraines, inflammation, pain, acne, wound healing, rosacea, skin irritation, fever, and digestive issues. This herbal preparation is commonly used in traditional medicine. Its common effect is anti-inflammatory, which is attributed to its ability to inhibit prostaglandin synthesis. Acmella oleracea tincture is typically made from the leaves and roots of the plant. The preparation requires careful processing, as the high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones can make it challenging to extract the desired compounds, making it a hard preparation to make.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Acmella oleracea leaves and roots, and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the leaves and roots in a cool, dark place, or using a dehydrator, to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried leaves and roots in a clean glass jar, and add a sufficient amount of 80% to 95% ethanol or glycerin as the solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar regularly, to allow the solvent to extract the plant's active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the herbal tincture.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation and swelling, and promote healing of wounds, minor burns, and skin irritations.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea salve is a herbal preparation used to treat a range of skin issues, including bruises, burns, wounds, skin irritations, insect bites, eczema, acne, and psoriasis. It's a widely used treatment in many cultures, often applied directly to affected areas to provide relief. The common effect of using Acmella oleracea salve is the reduction of inflammation and pain. The preparation is typically made from the leaves and roots of the Acmella oleracea plant, which are carefully harvested and then processed into a salve. However, the preparation is considered challenging to make due to the need for precise extraction and concentration of the active compounds, making it a time-consuming and labor-intensive process.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Acmella oleracea leaves and roots, and dry them properly for later use.
Combine the dried Acmella oleracea leaves and roots in a large pot, and cover them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil for several hours or overnight.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Add a preservative such as vitamin E oil to the infused oil, and mix well to create the Acmella oleracea herbal salve.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain and treat oral ulcers, as well as for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea essential oil is commonly used to treat pain, inflammation, skin irritation, wrinkles, acne, rosacea, muscle spasm, and eczema. It's a widely used preparation due to its proven efficacy in addressing various skin and muscle-related issues. Acmella oleracea essential oil has a common effect of reducing inflammation and relieving pain. This preparation is extracted from the flowers and leaves of the Acmella oleracea plant. The process of making this essential oil is challenging due to the delicate nature of the plant's components and the required precise distillation process to capture its active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Acmella oleracea flowers and leaves for distillation.
Wash the gathered flowers and leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any impurities.
Combine the flowers and leaves in a distillation apparatus, such as a steam distiller or a Clevenger apparatus.
Subject the mixture to gentle steam distillation to release the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect the resulting essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle to preserve its potency and aroma.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to treat toothache, mouth ulcers, and gum inflammation, as well as to enhance oral health and digestion.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea capsule is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including toothache, migraine, gingivitis, herpes, acne, canker sores and chapped lips. It's a common used remedy in traditional medicine, often found in many herbal stores and online marketplaces. The capsules typically have a numbing effect, which is its most common effect, due to the presence of spilanthes, a compound found in the leaves and roots of the Acmella oleracea plant. The preparation is usually made with the leaves and roots of the plant, which are collected, dried and then processed into a capsule form. Making Acmella oleracea capsule is moderately difficult, as it requires careful processing of the plant material to ensure the desired levels of spilanthes are maintained.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Acmella oleracea leaves and roots, and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the leaves and roots in a well-ventilated area, either air-drying or using a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried leaves and roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered leaves and roots in a specific ratio, usually 2:1 or 3:1, according to the desired herbal blend.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with the mixed powder, and seal them to create the Acmella oleracea herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat toothache, reduce inflammation, and relieve pain by numbing the mucous membranes and nerves.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea tea is a preparation used to treat gum inflammation, migraine, skin irritation, muscle pain, and toothache. It's a very common remedy due to its effectiveness, widely used in traditional medicine. The common effect of Acmella oleracea tea is its anti-inflammatory property, which helps to reduce swelling and pain. The preparation is made from the leaves and roots of the Acmella oleracea plant, which are combined and steeped in hot water to create the tea. Acmella oleracea tea is easy to make at home, requiring only a few minutes to prepare, making it a convenient and accessible treatment option.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Acmella oleracea leaves and roots from a trusted source.
Wash the leaves and roots thoroughly with clean water to remove any impurities.
Combine the cleaned leaves and roots in a large bowl and gently crush them to release their essential oils.
Transfer the crushed leaves and roots to a teapot and pour in boiling water to steep for several minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving the herbal tea.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used for treating oral inflammation, pain and ulcers, and for reducing swelling and inflammation in the mouth and throat.
preparations.preparation_overview: Acmella oleracea suppository is used to treat conditions such as hemorrhoids, prostatitis, proctitis, anal fissure, rectal pain, incontinence and constipation. This herbal suppository is relatively uncommon in medical practices due to its limited application and preparation process. The unique effect of Acmella oleracea suppository is its ability to reduce inflammation and pain in the affected areas without any noticeable side effects. The preparation is made from the leaves and roots of the plant, which are harvested and processed to create a concentrated form that can be used as a suppository. Acmella oleracea suppository is considered a challenging preparation due to the specific requirements for harvesting, drying and processing the plant material, making it difficult to produce in large quantities.
preparation_recipe: Dry the Acmella oleracea leaves and roots in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried leaves and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground leaves and roots with a small amount of vegetable oil to form a paste.
Add a small amount of natural wax, such as beeswax, to the paste to help hold its shape.
Fill the prepared suppository molds with the paste and allow them to solidify before use.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Acmella oleracea are experience allergic reactions and develop gastrointestinal disturbances, as improper use can trigger anaphylaxis and cause stomach pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, suffer from skin irritation and show increased heart rate are possible, as the plant's sap can cause skin redness and inflammation, and may lead to tachycardia in sensitive individuals. Experience dizziness and develop headaches are also potential outcomes, as the plant's volatile oils can cause dizziness, lightheadedness, and headaches due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, experience nausea and show decreased blood pressure are possible, as the plant's saponins and flavonoids can cause gastrointestinal upset and hypotension in some individuals. Experience respiratory problems and develop liver toxicity are the most severe possible outcomes, as the plant's volatile oils and saponins can cause bronchospasm and hepatotoxicity in rare cases.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Pregnant women, breastfeeding women, and people with kidney or liver disease should consult a healthcare professional before using Acmella oleracea.
Dosage should be established based on individual tolerance and specific use, typically starting with 1-5 drops of the plant's extract.
Consuming excessive amounts of Acmella oleracea may cause gastrointestinal irritation

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of interactions with other medications
precautions.precaution_list_description: Concurrent use with diabetes medications, blood thinners, and other medications may result in adverse effects, such as hypoglycemia or bleeding.

precautions.precaution_name: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate
precautions.precaution_list_description: Patients with hypertension or cardiovascular conditions should monitor blood pressure and heart rate due to Acmella oleracea's effect of increasing heart rate and blood pressure.

precautions.precaution_name: Use with caution in children and pregnant women
precautions.precaution_list_description: Children and pregnant women should avoid Acmella oleracea due to its lack of safety data in these populations.
Application to broken skin can lead to skin irritation.

precautions.precaution_name: Watch for signs of allergic reactions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Some individuals exhibit skin irritation, itching, or swelling in response to Acmella oleracea contact.

precautions.precaution_name: Store in a cool, dry place
precautions.precaution_list_description: Acmella oleracea should be stored in an environment with a temperature range between 15°C and 25°C and relative humidity below 60%

precautions.precaution_name: Follow proper preparation and handling procedures
precautions.precaution_list_description: Acmella oleracea requires thorough cleaning and handling to avoid contamination with bacteria and other pathogens.

properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Acmella oleracea are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, that inhibit the growth of various pathogens. The antioxidant properties of this plant are responsible for its ability to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby exerting anti-inflammatory effects and alleviating symptoms of pain and inflammation, which can be attributed to the antinociceptive properties of sesquiterpene lactones like acmellin. Moreover, Acmella oleracea has been found to exhibit analgesic and antibacterial activities, which can be attributed to the presence of volatile oils like beta-eudesmol and alpha-pinene. Its antiviral properties, on the other hand, are thought to be mediated by the saponins present in the plant, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viral particles. The hepatoprotective and anticancer properties of Acmella oleracea are also attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Acmella oleracea are primarily flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes. Saponins present in the plant possess antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms including bacteria and fungi, thereby aiding in wound healing and reducing the risk of infections. Glycosides specifically acmellin exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid found in Acmella oleracea exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties which contribute to its wound healing and anti-inflammatory activities. Alkaloids present in the plant such as acmellinine exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting pain pathways and reducing inflammation.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Acmella oleracea for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which contain concentrated extracts of the plant used to treat inflammation, wounds, and mouth sores. Acmella oleracea is also used in salve form to reduce inflammation and promote healing in topical applications. Essential oils derived from the plant are utilized for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties, particularly in oral health products. Capsules containing Acmella oleracea are used to treat digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. Additionally, the plant is prepared in a tea to relieve fever and as suppositories to treat anal fissures.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Acmella oleracea medicinally are consult a healthcare professional to ensure safe use, particularly for individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. To minimize risks, determine proper dosage based on age, weight, and health status, and avoid consuming in excess to prevent adverse effects such as gastrointestinal issues and allergic reactions. Be aware of interactions with other medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, as Acmella oleracea may enhance their effects. Monitor blood pressure and heart rate regularly during use, and use with caution in children and pregnant women due to limited research on its effects on fetal development. When applying Acmella oleracea topically, avoid applying to broken skin and watch for signs of allergic reactions, while storing it in a cool, dry place and following proper preparation and handling procedures to prevent contamination.
